tests.name: WBC
tests.what_it_measures: infection-fighting white blood cells
tests.high_meaning: may indicate infection, inflammation, stress response, or rarely leukemia
tests.low_meaning: may indicate bone marrow problems, autoimmune conditions, or severe infection

tests.name: RBC
tests.what_it_measures: oxygen-carrying red blood cells
tests.high_meaning: may indicate dehydration, lung disease, or polycythemia
tests.low_meaning: may indicate anemia, bleeding, or nutritional deficiency

tests.name: Hemoglobin
tests.what_it_measures: protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen
tests.high_meaning: may indicate dehydration or polycythemia
tests.low_meaning: indicates anemia — could be from bleeding, nutritional deficiency, or chronic disease

tests.name: Hematocrit
tests.what_it_measures: percentage of blood made up of red blood cells
tests.high_meaning: may indicate dehydration or polycythemia
tests.low_meaning: may indicate anemia or blood loss

tests.name: MCV
tests.what_it_measures: average size of red blood cells
tests.high_meaning: large red blood cells — may indicate B12 or folate deficiency, liver disease, or alcohol use
tests.low_meaning: small red blood cells — may indicate iron deficiency or thalassemia

tests.name: Platelets
tests.what_it_measures: blood cells that help with clotting
tests.high_meaning: may indicate inflammation, iron deficiency, or rarely a bone marrow disorder
tests.low_meaning: may increase bleeding risk — could be from medications, infection, or immune conditions

patterns.name: Iron Deficiency Anemia
patterns.description: Low hemoglobin with small red blood cells suggests iron deficiency

patterns.name: B12/Folate Deficiency
patterns.description: Low hemoglobin with large red blood cells suggests B12 or folate deficiency

patterns.name: Possible Infection
patterns.description: Elevated white blood cells may indicate active infection or inflammation

patterns.name: Pancytopenia
patterns.description: All cell lines low — warrants prompt evaluation for bone marrow disorders

tests.name: Glucose
tests.what_it_measures: blood sugar level
tests.high_meaning: may indicate diabetes or prediabetes
tests.low_meaning: may indicate hypoglycemia from medication, fasting, or other causes

tests.name: BUN
tests.what_it_measures: waste product filtered by kidneys
tests.high_meaning: may indicate dehydration, kidney problems, or high protein diet
tests.low_meaning: usually not clinically significant

tests.name: Creatinine
tests.what_it_measures: waste product from muscle metabolism filtered by kidneys
tests.high_meaning: may indicate kidney dysfunction
tests.low_meaning: may indicate low muscle mass

tests.name: Sodium
tests.what_it_measures: electrolyte important for fluid balance and nerve function
tests.high_meaning: may indicate dehydration
tests.low_meaning: may indicate overhydration, certain medications, or hormonal issues

tests.name: Potassium
tests.what_it_measures: electrolyte critical for heart and muscle function
tests.high_meaning: can affect heart rhythm — may be from kidney problems or medications
tests.low_meaning: can affect heart rhythm — may be from diuretics, vomiting, or diarrhea

tests.name: Chloride
tests.what_it_measures: electrolyte that helps maintain fluid balance
tests.high_meaning: may indicate dehydration or kidney problems
tests.low_meaning: may indicate overhydration or prolonged vomiting

tests.name: CO2
tests.what_it_measures: acid-base balance in blood
tests.high_meaning: may indicate metabolic alkalosis
tests.low_meaning: may indicate metabolic acidosis

tests.name: Calcium
tests.what_it_measures: mineral important for bones, muscles, and nerves
tests.high_meaning: may indicate parathyroid problems, vitamin D excess, or cancer
tests.low_meaning: may indicate vitamin D deficiency, parathyroid problems, or kidney disease

tests.name: Total Protein
tests.what_it_measures: total protein in blood including albumin and globulins
tests.high_meaning: may indicate dehydration, chronic inflammation, or liver disease
tests.low_meaning: may indicate malnutrition, liver disease, or kidney disease

tests.name: Albumin
tests.what_it_measures: protein made by the liver — helps assess nutrition and liver function
tests.high_meaning: usually indicates dehydration
tests.low_meaning: may indicate liver disease, kidney disease, or malnutrition

tests.name: ALT
tests.what_it_measures: liver enzyme
tests.high_meaning: may indicate liver inflammation, medication effect, or liver disease
tests.low_meaning: normal — not clinically significant

tests.name: AST
tests.what_it_measures: enzyme found in liver, heart, and muscles
tests.high_meaning: may indicate liver damage, muscle injury, or heart damage
tests.low_meaning: normal — not clinically significant

tests.name: ALP
tests.what_it_measures: enzyme from liver and bones
tests.high_meaning: may indicate bile duct obstruction, bone disorders, or liver disease
tests.low_meaning: may indicate malnutrition or zinc deficiency

tests.name: Bilirubin
tests.what_it_measures: waste product from breakdown of red blood cells
tests.high_meaning: may cause jaundice — could indicate liver disease, bile duct obstruction, or hemolysis
tests.low_meaning: not clinically significant

patterns.name: Kidney Dysfunction
patterns.description: Both BUN and creatinine elevated suggests impaired kidney function

patterns.name: Hepatocellular Injury
patterns.description: Elevated liver enzymes with normal ALP suggests direct liver cell damage

patterns.name: Cholestatic Pattern
patterns.description: Elevated ALP with bilirubin suggests bile duct obstruction

patterns.name: Diabetes Risk
patterns.description: Elevated fasting glucose warrants A1C testing and diabetes evaluation

patterns.name: Electrolyte Imbalance
patterns.description: Electrolyte abnormalities may need medication review and monitoring

tests.name: Total Cholesterol
tests.what_it_measures: total cholesterol in blood
tests.high_meaning: increased cardiovascular risk
tests.low_meaning: not typically a concern unless very low

tests.name: LDL
tests.what_it_measures: cholesterol that builds up in artery walls
tests.high_meaning: increased risk of heart disease and stroke
tests.low_meaning: generally favorable

tests.name: HDL
tests.what_it_measures: protective cholesterol that helps remove LDL
tests.high_meaning: generally protective — higher is better
tests.low_meaning: increased cardiovascular risk

tests.name: Triglycerides
tests.what_it_measures: fat in blood from food and liver production
tests.high_meaning: may increase cardiovascular risk and pancreatitis risk if very high
tests.low_meaning: not typically a concern

patterns.name: High Cardiovascular Risk
patterns.description: Classic atherogenic lipid profile — warrants aggressive risk management

patterns.name: Metabolic Syndrome Pattern
patterns.description: This combination is associated with metabolic syndrome and insulin resistance

tests.name: TSH
tests.what_it_measures: hormone that controls thyroid gland activity
tests.high_meaning: thyroid is underactive (hypothyroidism) — brain is telling thyroid to work harder
tests.low_meaning: thyroid may be overactive (hyperthyroidism) — brain is telling thyroid to slow down

tests.name: Free T4
tests.what_it_measures: main hormone produced by thyroid
tests.high_meaning: thyroid is producing too much hormone (hyperthyroidism)
tests.low_meaning: thyroid is not producing enough hormone (hypothyroidism)

tests.name: Free T3
tests.what_it_measures: active form of thyroid hormone
tests.high_meaning: may indicate hyperthyroidism
tests.low_meaning: may indicate hypothyroidism or sick euthyroid syndrome

patterns.name: Primary Hypothyroidism
patterns.description: High TSH with low T4 confirms underactive thyroid

patterns.name: Subclinical Hypothyroidism
patterns.description: Mildly elevated TSH with normal T4 — may need monitoring or treatment depending on level

patterns.name: Hyperthyroidism
patterns.description: Low TSH with high T4 confirms overactive thyroid

patterns.name: Subclinical Hyperthyroidism
patterns.description: Low TSH with normal T4 — warrants monitoring

tests.name: HbA1c
tests.what_it_measures: average blood sugar over the past 2-3 months
tests.high_meaning: 5.7-6.4% indicates prediabetes; 6.5% or higher indicates diabetes; above 9% indicates poor diabetes control
tests.low_meaning: not typically a concern unless very low with symptoms

patterns.name: Well-Controlled Diabetes
patterns.description: A1C under 7% generally indicates good diabetes management

patterns.name: Poorly Controlled Diabetes
patterns.description: A1C above 9% indicates need for medication adjustment and lifestyle changes

tests.name: pH
tests.what_it_measures: acidity or alkalinity of urine
tests.high_meaning: may indicate UTI, kidney problems, or diet effects
tests.low_meaning: may indicate metabolic acidosis or high-protein diet

tests.name: Specific Gravity
tests.what_it_measures: how concentrated the urine is
tests.high_meaning: concentrated urine — may indicate dehydration
tests.low_meaning: dilute urine — may indicate overhydration or kidney issues

tests.name: Protein
tests.what_it_measures: protein that shouldn't normally be in urine
tests.high_meaning: may indicate kidney damage, especially in diabetics
tests.low_meaning: normal

tests.name: Glucose
tests.what_it_measures: sugar in urine — normally absent
tests.high_meaning: may indicate uncontrolled diabetes
tests.low_meaning: normal

tests.name: WBC
tests.what_it_measures: white blood cells in urine
tests.high_meaning: suggests urinary tract infection or inflammation
tests.low_meaning: normal

tests.name: Bacteria
tests.what_it_measures: bacteria in urine — normally absent in clean catch
tests.high_meaning: suggests urinary tract infection
tests.low_meaning: normal

patterns.name: Urinary Tract Infection
patterns.description: White cells and bacteria in urine are consistent with UTI

patterns.name: Diabetic Nephropathy Screen
patterns.description: Protein and glucose in urine of diabetic patient warrants kidney function evaluation
